response: LoRA stands for Low-Rank Adaptation. It is a memory-efficient fine-tuning method that freezes the weight quantization of pretrained weights and learns low-rank adapter matrices instead. This approach reduces the memory footprint of fine-tuning by orders of magnitude compared to full fine-tuning.

DPO stands for Divergence from Reference (Divergence from Desirable Behavior in the case of DPO-GPT) — a preference-based alignment method that optimizes a model to produce responses closer to chosen reference responses than to chosen rejected responses. It is often used for safety-critical fine-tun
QLoRA stands for Quantized Low-Rank Adaptation. It is a memory-efficient fine-tuning method that quantizes the fine-tuning adapter and the base model's frozen layers to 4-bit precision.
RLHF stands for Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback. It is an alignment method that uses reward models to learn from human preferences.

GRPO stands for Gradient Reversal Proxy Optimization. It is a preference-based alignment method that avoids the need for a reference model by directly optimizing the model's own outputs against chosen preference pairs.

PagedAttention (PagedAttention) is a memory-efficient attention implementation that reduces GPU memory spikes during attention computation. It uses a paged cache to store attention weights, reducing the need for full-matrix allocation. This improves inference throughput on GPUs with limited memory.
